reasoning: According to the 2023 Access to Financial Services in Nigeria survey by EFInA (the primary data source for the Central Bank of Nigeria), formal financial inclusion—which includes bank accounts, mobile money, and other regulated services—rose to 64% of the adult population. The total financial inclusion rate (including informal services) was 74% in late 2023. Both figures are significantly below the 95% target. Furthermore, Bank Verification Number (BVN) registration data, which is mandatory for all bank accounts in Nigeria, stood at roughly 61.5 million in April 2024 and 64.8 million by January 2025. Given an adult population (18+) estimated at approximately 115-125 million, this represents a banking penetration rate of roughly 50-55%. There is no evidence of a massive surge in the final months of 2024 that could have bridged the ~40 percentage point gap to reach 95%. News reports throughout late 2023 and 2024 consistently describe the country as being 'far from' the 95% goal.
